capability_statement: Operators can inspect whether provider production execution is merely a candidate, blocked, or missing required pre-production boundaries before any real provider operation is considered.
before_state: AOF had approval, reproduction, rollback, outcome, learning, operator acceptance, and value evidence, but no single boundary packet explained whether production execution was eligible.
after_state: AOF has a provider production boundary candidate packet and audit that bundles upstream evidence and blocks production execution authorization in v9.2.
scenario: Before attempting a real GitHub/Jira/Slack/OpenAI provider write, an operator runs provider-production-boundary-audit and sees credential, budget, revocation, rollback, monitoring, incident, human go/no-go, and value-comprehension boundaries.
five_minute_demo: Open docs/v9.2-release-definition.md, then run provider-production-boundary-audit and release-state-audit; confirm production_execution_authorized_count is 0.
time_saved_or_work_reduced: Operators no longer have to manually inspect seven separate provider artifacts to know whether production execution is blocked.
cognitive_load_removed: The distinction between pre-production eligibility and production safety is explicit instead of inferred from green audits.

capability_matrix.before: manual cross-reading of external runtime artifacts
capability_matrix.after: single boundary record/audit with explicit no-production-authority rule
capability_matrix.user_value: safer provider execution planning without overclaiming production safety

not_proven: This proves release value explanation structure only, not production safety or market adoption.
